questions.q: To which city did Rama return after his exile?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya was Rama's home and the capital of the Kosala kingdom to which he returned.

questions.q: How many years did Rama spend in exile before returning to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama was sentenced to fourteen years of forest exile, after which he returned home.

questions.q: Which festival is traditionally said to commemorate Rama's return to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: The lighting of lamps to welcome Rama is remembered ever after as Diwali, the festival of lights.

questions.q: What did the people of Ayodhya light to welcome Rama home?
questions.explanation: The citizens lit rows of lamps (deepas) to welcome Rama, giving rise to the festival of lights.

questions.q: Who returned to Ayodhya alongside Rama?
questions.explanation: Rama returned with his wife Sita and his devoted brother Lakshmana, who had shared the exile with him.

questions.q: Which brother had ruled Ayodhya in Rama's absence and awaited his return?
questions.explanation: Bharata governed Ayodhya as regent during the exile, ruling in Rama's name while awaiting his return.

questions.q: What ceremony was held for Rama after his return to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama was crowned king of Ayodhya, an event celebrated as his coronation (rajyabhisheka).

questions.q: From which island kingdom was Rama returning when he came back to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama returned after defeating Ravana and rescuing Sita from the island kingdom of Lanka.

questions.q: What was Rama's mood and the city's mood upon his return?
questions.explanation: The homecoming was a joyful, celebratory event, with the whole city rejoicing at Rama's return.

questions.q: Who was the great devotee and monkey ally who accompanied Rama on the journey back?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, the devoted vanara, accompanied Rama and even flew ahead to announce his coming.

questions.q: On what flying vehicle did Rama travel back to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama returned aboard the Pushpaka Vimana, the flying chariot taken from Ravana.

questions.q: From whom had the Pushpaka Vimana originally been seized before Ravana possessed it?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana belonged to Kubera, the god of wealth, from whom Ravana had taken it by force.

questions.q: Whom did Rama send ahead to Bharata to announce his return before he himself arrived?
questions.explanation: Rama sent Hanuman ahead to Bharata to bring word of his return and gauge Bharata's heart.

questions.q: What had Bharata vowed to do if Rama did not return on the very day the exile ended?
questions.explanation: Bharata had vowed to immolate himself in fire if Rama failed to return when the fourteen years ended.

questions.q: Where had Bharata kept Rama's sandals during the exile as a symbol of Rama's authority?
questions.explanation: Bharata placed Rama's wooden sandals (paduka) on the throne and ruled in their name, not as king himself.

questions.q: At which place near Ayodhya did Bharata go to meet the returning Rama?
questions.explanation: Bharata had been living at Nandigrama as an ascetic and went there and onward to receive Rama.

questions.q: Which allies from Lanka and the forest accompanied Rama as honored guests on the return?
options: Vibhishana, Sugriva, and the vanara chiefs
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Sugriva, Hanuman, and other vanara chiefs accompanied Rama to Ayodhya and witnessed his coronation.

questions.q: Whom did Rama ask the gods to revive before he departed Lanka, so they could share in the homecoming?
options: The fallen vanara and rksha warriors
questions.explanation: Indra granted Rama's wish to restore the fallen monkey and bear warriors to life so they could return joyfully.

questions.q: During the flight home, Rama pointed out to Sita the sites of his adventures, including the bridge to Lanka. What was that bridge called?
questions.explanation: Rama showed Sita the Rama Setu, the great causeway his army had built across the ocean to Lanka.

questions.q: Which sage's hermitage did Rama visit on the way back, where Bharata's regency was confirmed?
questions.explanation: Rama halted at the hermitage of the sage Bharadvaja, from where he sent Hanuman ahead to Bharata.

questions.q: Who performed Rama's coronation ceremony in Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha, the royal preceptor and family priest, consecrated Rama as king of Ayodhya.

questions.q: In tradition, the joyous reign that began with Rama's coronation is known by what name?
questions.explanation: Rama's just and prosperous rule is celebrated as Rama Rajya, the ideal kingdom of righteousness.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, in which kanda (book) does the return to Ayodhya and coronation principally occur?
questions.explanation: The return flight, reunion with Bharata, and coronation are narrated in the Yuddha Kanda, the war book that closes the original epic.

questions.q: Why did Rama insist on returning to Ayodhya in such haste, even by air, rather than a slow overland march?
options: Because the fourteen years were ending and Bharata's vow of self-immolation loomed
questions.explanation: The exile term was expiring and Rama hurried lest Bharata, true to his vow, immolate himself if Rama did not appear in time.

questions.q: After bearing Rama home, what did Rama do with the Pushpaka Vimana?
options: Kept it as a royal vehicle
Returned it to its rightful owner Kubera
questions.explanation: Rama dismissed the Pushpaka and bade it return to Kubera, its rightful owner, honoring its dharma.

questions.q: Which queen-mothers of Ayodhya greeted Rama, Bharata, and the brothers upon the return?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha's three widowed queens, Kausalya, Sumitra, and Kaikeyi, were present to receive the returning princes.

questions.q: What gesture marked Bharata formally handing the kingdom back to Rama?
options: He placed Rama's sandals on Rama's feet
questions.explanation: Bharata reverently restored Rama's sandals to his feet, surrendering the regency he had held only in trust.

questions.q: According to tradition, after the coronation Rama gave generous gifts and bade farewell to his allies. Who received a celestial necklace from Sita as a special token?
questions.explanation: Sita gave Hanuman a precious pearl necklace as a mark of gratitude for his unmatched devotion and service.

questions.q: Before re-entering the city, Rama and his companions paused so the travel-worn could be refreshed and made fit for the capital. Where did the party first halt outside Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: The party halted at Nandigrama, where Bharata had dwelt, before the grand entry into Ayodhya for the coronation.

questions.q: In the traditional reckoning, Rama's return and coronation are said to take place in which yuga (cosmic age)?
questions.explanation: Rama is regarded as an avatar of Vishnu in the Treta Yuga, the age in which the events of the Ramayana unfold.
